title: What nobody tells you about building a profitable BTC algo
author: Spirited_Syllabub488
selftext:
20 months. $2,100. Countless mistakes.

That's what building a profitable BTC algo actually cost me. Nobody talks about this part.

**The time nobody tells you about:** I spent 2 months just researching before writing a single line of code. 13 months coding. 4 months breaking it and fixing it in backtests. Then sat and watched it for a month before trusting it with real money.

**The money nobody tells you about:** Data was free. Everything else wasn't. VPS, fees, books, subscriptions — $1,700 gone before going live. Then the mistakes added another $400 on top.

Over-optimized my first version. Watched it fail live. Ignored slippage, then got wrecked on fills. Panicked during a drawdown, shut it down early, and lastly missed the entire recovery period as turned off the model. Then broke the system by tweaking it when I should've left it alone.

$2,100 and many months of lessons I didn't expect to pay for.

**So was it worth it?**

100% YES. I haven't made back what I spent yet. But I have something most people don't — a system that's been live 6 months, up +40%, while BTC is down 50% in the same period.

That's the asset. Not the returns. The system itself.

If you're thinking about building one — go in knowing it will take longer and cost more than you think. The only thing that actually matters once it's live is leaving it alone.

That's the part that almost broke me.

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body:
It depends on where you are currently right now.

If you are completely new the take my honest opinion, don't fall for GURUS unless they show you their data.

Using data, ML and such stuff in trading is completely different from what we see on social media. 

It's a completely different perspective of trading. 

Start with learning data analysis and data mining. And then  try to make some simple algo models (i dont care whether that is unpfotiable or not, just code it), then start with learning ML models and their use cases, like how can you use such model in your strategy in which way........

  
You will learn all these through the path.. Just dont fall for any GURUS showing lambos.  
Trust me I learned alone and built my profitable model all from scratch.

You can do it too. Best of luck.

replies.author: jonno85
replies.body:
Interesting, any more detailed guidelines?
Like platform, models, provider and such?

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body: You can start with simple supervised models and then come to NN, LSTM and such models.....

replies.author: Economy_Fox_8866
replies.body: What’s the point of telling this you cannot actually give any specific information?

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body:
I cannot give you a proper checklist, but I can show you the way, which I did.

You have to figure out your own checklists, even if I give you what exactly I did you cannot learn anything. As your mind will position 'my roadmap' as a hard work and not an exciting journey.

comments.author: sonzvek
comments.body:
Hey ! Thanks for the feedback. 
I just started training a model from scratch. Idea is to build the system and learn ML/AI conception and design. But I feel I am spending too much time on : ML architecture ( transformer, LSTM etc ) in addition to mathematical concepts and traning specs. Do you recommend following this path, or use existing and performant models out there, and build around it.
Thanks !

replies.author: ChainPlastic7530
replies.body: data seems far more important than model

comments.author: AttitudeGrouchy33
comments.body:
The "leaving it alone" part is real. The urge to tweak after every bad day is almost impossible to resist, and that's usually when systems die.



One thing that helped me think about this differently: the algo isn't really the edge, the execution layer is. The strategy itself is perishable, basically an app running on top of more durable infrastructure. Sizing rules, drawdown limits, the logic for when to sit out entirely. That stuff stays consistent even as the signal decays.



When you built that mental separation between the system and the strategy, it gets easier to retire a strategy without feeling like you're killing the whole thing. You're just swapping the app.



If you're curious how others are approaching this for crypto specifically, worth checking out app.andmilo.com/?code=@milo4aitrading. Different take on the same problem.

comments.author: Backtester4Ever
comments.body:
This is one of the more honest posts I’ve seen.

Most people underestimate two things: how long it takes to kill bad ideas, and how hard it is not to interfere once something is live. The money you spent wasn’t on code. It was on learning execution reality and behavioral control.

The real test now isn’t +40% over 6 months. It’s whether the system survives a full regime shift without you touching it. In proper research workflows, including in tools like WealthLab, most of the work is exactly what you described: breaking the model on purpose before the market does.

If it’s truly robust, the hardest part going forward won’t be building it. It’ll be not “improving” it to death.This is actually one of the more honest posts I’ve seen.

Two years, a couple thousand dollars, multiple failures, slippage pain, over optimization. That is normal. Anyone who says it is easier is either new or selling something.

The real lesson is not the +40% in six months. It is that you learned that backtests lie when fills are ideal, slippage matters more than you think, tweaking during drawdowns kills systems, and discipline after deployment is harder than coding.

That is the real edge most people never build.

Six months live is promising, not proof. Let it run through ugly conditions. Document everything. Do not scale too fast.

The system is the asset, but only if it survives time.

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body:
Absolutely. 

I am running it on my limited capital now and forming my track record. At least 1 year should be enough I guess, if it generates alpha over BTC spot.

Once it proves its profitability over the BTC spot, I will look for investors.

I created a Reddit account around 15 mins ago FOR my sentiment trading system. Thought I’d reach out and hopefully we can get a chat going and share ideas 👍🏼

replies.body: Yeah sure, there is no problem from my side.
